steps.text: The function is $K(v) = \frac{7}{2}v^2$, where $v$ is speed in meters per second and $K(v)$ is kinetic energy in joules. The notation $K(24) = 2{,}016$ means the input to the function is $v = 24$ and the output is $K = 2{,}016$.
The input $v = 24$ represents a speed of 24 meters per second, and the output $K = 2{,}016$ represents a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules.

• Choice A: "The object traveling at 24 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules." This matches $v = 24$ and $K = 2{,}016$ exactly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • Choice B: "The object traveling at 240 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 2,016 joules." The speed is 24, not 240. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: "The object traveling at 2,016 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules." The roles of input and output are reversed. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: "The object traveling at 7,056 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 24 joules." Neither value corresponds to the function notation correctly. Eliminated.
